subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request from u_9981 (senior-engineer) to db-payments-primary against access control and incident response policies. Verify: (1) user role authorization for production database access, (2) P1 incident justification validity, (3) emergency access exception criteria, (4) audit/approval requirements.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask (mid tier) because the query explicitly requests governance evaluation of emergency access against policies. This requires multi-signal reasoning: checking user role entitlements, incident severity classification, emergency access exception policy, and approval workflows. Mid tier is appropriate because this involves multiple policy conditions (role + incident justification + access scope) interacting together, requiring moderate reasoning complexity. No additional subtasks needed — access approval/denial and compliance determination are within policy_action scope. No cross_recon needed as this is a single-source policy decision, not a multi-source reconciliation."

output.reason: User u_9981 (senior-engineer) holds an active P1 incident ticket (INC-20241215-0042), satisfying POL-501's exception clause for non-DBA roles. POL-502 dual-approval requirement is waived under declared P1 incident status, but access is conditional on incident ticket remaining active and valid. POL-503 requires this emergency grant to be fully logged with a mandatory post-incident review within 24 hours of access termination.
applied_constraints: Access scope limited to read_write operations directly relevant to P1 incident investigation on db-payments-primary only.
Emergency access session must be logged in full with timestamps, queries executed, and session recording enabled.
Access grant automatically expires upon P1 incident closure or after 4 hours, whichever comes first, requiring re-authorization for extension.
Incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 must remain in active P1 status for duration of access; ticket closure triggers immediate access revocation.
Post-incident audit review must be completed within 24 hours of access termination and submitted to security and DBA teams.
User u_9981 must not share, delegate, or transfer the emergency access grant to any other user or process.
Any data modifications must be documented in incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 in real time.
